title: 10 best herbal creams for amenorrhea

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong Quai's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and amenorrhea by promoting a healthy environment for hormone production.

Vasodilatory properties: Dong Quai's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve circulation, which can increase blood flow to the uterus and ovaries, supporting hormonal balance and regular menstrual cycles.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Dong Quai contains isoflavones that can help regulate estrogen levels in the body, which can be beneficial for women with amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or deficiencies.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, creams helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to balance and regulate hormones in the body. The cream's active compounds, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, stimulate the pituitary gland to release follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), which are essential for menstrual cycle regulation. Additionally, dong quai's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and reducing the risk of amenorrhea. This natural remedy supports menstrual health and fertility.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly ferulic acid and caffeic acid, help regulate menstrual cycles by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, promoting regular menstrual flow.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, loganin and secologanin): These compounds may help stimulate uterine contractions and regulate hormone balance, leading to improved menstrual regularity and reduced symptoms of amenorrhea.

Coumarins (specifically, scopoletin and scopoletin glucoside): These compounds may help regulate hormone balance, reduce inflammation, and promote uterine contractions, all of which can contribute to the normalization of menstrual cycles and relief from amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of ferulic acid, which has been shown to stimulate uterine contractions and promote menstrual flow.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have a regulatory effect on the menstrual cycle and help alleviate menstrual irregularities.
Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have a regulatory effect on the menstrual cycle and promote menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 10 grams of dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Vasoprotective effects: Chaste tree cream's vasoprotective effects help to strengthen and stabilize blood vessels, which can improve uterine blood flow and hormonal regulation, thereby addressing underlying causes of amenorrhea.

Hormone-balancing properties: The cream contains compounds that have a regulating effect on the endocrine system, particularly on estrogen and progesterone levels, helping to restore normal menstrual cycles in cases of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, creams helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to regulate hormonal imbalances. It contains a compound called aucubin, which helps to stimulate the production of luteinizing hormone (LH), a key hormone involved in ovulation and menstrual cycles. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, Vitex agnus castus creams can help to restore regular menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. This makes it a popular natural remedy for women experiencing menstrual irregularities.
remedy_constituents: Agnuside: Agnuside is a diterpenoid compound found in Vitex agnus castus, which helps regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea by stimulating the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), thereby promoting ovulation and menstrual flow.

Agnuscastin: Agnuscastin is a flavonoid glycoside present in Vitex agnus castus, which helps regulate hormonal balance and alleviate amenorrhea by modulating the activity of GnRH and LH, ultimately leading to the normalization of menstrual cycles.

Casticin: Casticin is a flavonoid compound found in Vitex agnus castus, which helps alleviate amenorrhea by stimulating the release of GnRH and LH, promoting ovulation, and regulating menstrual cycles due to its estrogenic and progesterogenic activities.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of iridoid glycosides, which are believed to have a regulating effect on hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: They are utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and balance the body's systems.
Seeds: They are employed for their hormonal balancing effects, particularly in regulating estrogen levels and menstrual cycles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried vitex agnus castus flowers from a trusted source to ensure quality and potency.
Combine the dried flowers with 20ml of carrier oil in a glass jar to create the infusion.
Place the jar in a warm dark place and let it steep for 2 weeks to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into another glass container discarding the solids to produce the herbal infusion.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 10ml of coconut oil and the herbal infusion in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to treat amenorrhea due to their ability to regulate hormones and improve menstrual flow.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root, dried to 10 moisture content for best results.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder at 10 seconds per gram.
Combine 500 grams of distilled water with 100 grams of the ground root powder in a stainless steel vessel.
Heat the mixture to 90 degrees celsius, then reduce heat to 60 degrees celsius and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and add 20 grams of beeswax to thicken.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried root of althaea officinalis in the spring season when it is in full bloom.
Combine the harvested root with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes to extract the root's medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of emulsifying wax with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax to create a cream base.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 100 grams of vegetable oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.

Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

Blend the infused oil mixture with the melted wax mixture and pour into small containers for storage and application.

Combine the ginkgo biloba powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves, wash them gently and chop into small pieces.
Combine 20 grams of chopped melissa leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days to allow for gentle extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then mix in 10 grams of beeswax pellets with 20 milliliters of coconut oil.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and melt at low temperature (30-40°c) for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed creams helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate hormonal balance in the body. The phytoestrogens present in milkweed, particularly isoflavones, mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. Additionally, milkweed's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce uterine inflammation and promote a healthy endometrial lining, further supporting menstrual regularity. By promoting hormonal balance and reducing inflammation, herbal milkweed creams can help restore normal menstruation patterns in women with amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Milkweed cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cardenolides, help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, which can contribute to amenorrhea.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of milkweed cream, including flavonoids and saponins, can help protect the body from oxidative stress and promote healthy hormone balance, which may aid in regulating menstruation and alleviating symptoms of amenorrhea.

Hormone-regulating properties: Milkweed contains compounds like cardenolides that have been shown to modulate hormonal activity, including estrogen levels, which is essential for regulating the menstrual cycle and treating conditions like amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Milkweed roots contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing menstrual cramps and reducing inflammation associated with amenorrhea.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of milkweed plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to regulate hormones and improve uterine blood flow, making them useful for treating amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried asclepias syriaca root powder from a reputable supplier.
Combine the harvested powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally, to facilitate infusion.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it steep for an additional 1 hour.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams help with amenorrhea because they contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can ease hormonal imbalances and alleviate stress, two common underlying causes of this condition. The calming effects of chamomile can also help regulate the menstrual cycle, promoting healthy ovulation and reducing symptoms such as cramps and mood swings. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect the uterus and promote overall reproductive health, making it a natural and effective solution for women experiencing amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce pelvic congestion and cramping associated with menstrual irregularities, which can contribute to amenorrhea.
Sedative property: The sedative nature of chamomile cream can calm the uterine muscle, regulating hormonal balance and promoting a normal menstrual cycle, thereby treating amenorrhea.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to hormone imbalance and menstrual irregularities, including amenorrhea, by protecting the body from free radicals and promoting overall reproductive health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin and luteolin, the flowers help reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, alleviating menstrual cramps and promoting regular menstruation.
Roots: Containing high amounts of flavonoids and coumarins, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe pelvic pain and regulate hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and combine them with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 3 hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil has infused with the chamomilla's volatile compounds.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Combine 20g of beeswax pellets with 10ml of the infused oil in a small saucepan and heat gently over low heat until melted and smooth.
Pour the melted wax-oil mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before sealing tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with amenorrhea because they promote a sense of calmness and relaxation in the body. Amenorrhea is often linked to hormonal imbalances, stress, and anxiety, which can disrupt menstrual cycles. The soothing properties of lavender help to regulate hormones, reduce anxiety and stress levels, and improve sleep quality. By creating a tranquil environment for the body, English lavender creams can encourage the return of menstruation by normalizing hormone production and promoting a healthy reproductive cycle. This natural approach can be particularly beneficial for women experiencing amenorrhea due to emotional or psychological factors.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Lavender's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to Amenorrhea by addressing underlying hormonal imbalances.

Hormone Regulation: The estrogens in lavender have been shown to regulate hormones and balance menstrual cycles, helping to alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea such as irregular periods or absent periods.

Stress Relief and Calming Effects: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for Amenorrhea. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, lavender creams may help restore normal menstrual function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and ease symptoms associated with amenorrhea due to their rich content of phytoestrogens, which help balance hormone levels.
Barks: The bark of the English lavender plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers, weighing approximately 200 grams for this recipe.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 1-2 hours.
Combine dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 milliliters of sweet almond oil and let it infuse for 2 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids after 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax, melting it in a double boiler then stirring until combined and cooled completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to stimulate hormonal balance. The creams' primary ingredient, beach rose extract, has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea such as irregular periods or complete cessation of menstruation. Additionally, the creams may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for amenorrhea. By addressing these underlying issues, beach rose creams can help restore a healthy menstrual cycle and promote overall reproductive well-being.
remedy_properties: Emmenagogic properties: Beach rose creams have emmenagogic properties, which means they can stimulate menstrual flow and relieve delayed or absent menstruation (amenorrhea) by increasing uterine contractions and improving blood circulation to the reproductive organs.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in beach rose creams help relax the uterus and alleviate cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with amenorrhea, making it easier for the body to initiate menstruation.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Beach rose creams may also help regulate estrogen levels, which can become imbalanced in cases of amenorrhea. By normalizing estrogen production, beach rose creams may help stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to regulate hormonal balance and ease menstrual cramps.
Barks: Contains phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pelvic pain and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh rosa rugosa petals, ensuring they are free from pesticides and heavy metals.
Dry the petals in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or air-dry them for 7 days.
Combine 100g of dried rosa rugosa petals with 300ml of organic coconut oil and infuse for 14 days.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth, retaining the oil and discarding the solids, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter, heating at 60°c until smooth and creamy.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for amenorrhea are topical preparations containing plant extracts that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. These creams are a natural and non-invasive approach to treating amenorrhea, which is characterized by the absence of menstrual periods. The best herbal creams for amenorrhea include Angelica sinensis, Paeonia lactiflora, Astragalus membranaceus, Cimicifuga racemosa, Vitex agnus-castus, Panax ginseng, Rehmannia glutinosa, Cuscuta chinensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, amenorrhea can lead to infertility, mood swings, and hormonal imbalances, significantly impacting one's quality of life, causing emotional distress, and affecting relationships and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to amenorrhea that are treated with herbal creams are endometriosis, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), and dysmenorrhea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like chasteberry, Dong quai, and wild yam help alleviate symptoms such as irregular periods, heavy bleeding, and pelvic pain by promoting hormonal balance and improving uterine health. These creams can be used in conjunction with conventional treatments to provide natural relief from menstrual disorders.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with amenorrhea is a blend of Clary Sage, Peppermint, and Wild Yam. Clary Sage helps to regulate hormones and reduce stress, while Peppermint aids in digestion and circulation, which can improve menstrual flow. Wild Yam contains diosgenin, a plant-based estrogen that can stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. These creams can be applied topically to the lower abdomen or taken internally as a supplement, but consult a healthcare professional before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis creams helps with amenorrhea because they are rich in bioactive compounds, particularly ferulic acid and ligustilide, which have been shown to stimulate ovarian function and regulate menstrual cycles. These compounds may also improve uterine contractions, thereby facilitating the release of an egg. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Angelica sinensis may help to reduce stress and hormonal imbalances that can contribute to amenorrhea, promoting a more regular and healthy menstrual cycle.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help regulate menstrual cycles by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, thereby promoting a healthy endometrial lining and normal menstrual function.
Furanocoumarins: These compounds have estrogen-modulating effects, which can help balance hormonal imbalances that may contribute to amenorrhea, thereby promoting regular menstrual cycles.

Coumarins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce uterine inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting a healthy uterine environment and regular menstrual function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root of Angelica sinensis is rich in ferulic acid and other compounds that can help to regulate menstrual cycles.
Buds: Angelica sinensis buds contain flavonoids and other phytochemicals that may help to balance hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual regularity.
Stems: The stems of Angelica sinensis contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that may help to stimulate blood flow and reduce menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Dry angelica sinensis roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 20 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder to increase surface area.
Combine the ground powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 minutes while stirring constantly to ensure even melting of the wax.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and solidify before transferring it to a container for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain the active compound paeoniflorin, which has been found to stimulate uterine contractions and regulate menstrual cycles. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a healthy environment for menstrual flow. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora creams have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to regulate the body's energy and balance the menstrual cycle, making it a promising natural remedy for women experiencing amenorrhea. Saponins: Saponins in Paeonia lactiflora help regulate the endocrine system and promote hormonal balance, which can help stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating properties, which can help promote menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms by regulating hormonal imbalances and promoting uterine health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Paeonia lactiflora are used in creams for amenorrhea due to their potential to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.
Flowers: The flowers of Paeonia lactiflora are used in creams for amenorrhea due to their estrogenic properties, which can help stimulate menstrual flow and regulate the menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep the roots in 200ml of hot distilled water for 30 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion and combine it with 20g of beeswax and 50g of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax melts and blends.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams helps with amenorrhea because of its adaptogenic properties, which aid in regulating hormonal balance and stimulating the reproductive system. The herb's ability to increase cortisol levels and boost energy can help alleviate stress-related amenorrhea. Astragalus membranaceus also contains saponins and polysaccharides that may stimulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, promoting menstrual cycle regulation and potentially leading to the return of menstruation in individuals experiencing amenorrhea.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help regulate menstrual cycles by modulating hormonal balance and improving uterine function.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system, promoting regular menstrual cycles.
Phenylethanoid Glycosides: Stimulate uterine contractions, promote menstrual flow, and help regulate hormonal balance, contributing to the treatment of amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in saponins, which are believed to have estrogenic properties that can help regulate menstrual cycles.
Rhyzomes: They contain bioactive compounds that may help stimulate blood flow and improve hormonal balance in the body, leading to regular menstrual cycles.
Seeds: They are rich in astragalosides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried astragalus membranaceus root in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids then combine it with 1/2 cup of distilled water.
Melt 2 ounces of beeswax in a double boiler and add 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Combine the infused liquid mixture and the melted wax mixture then stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain the active compound actein, which has been shown to regulate menstrual cycles and stimulate uterine contractions. This herb has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat menstrual disorders, including amenorrhea. Studies have found that Cimicifuga racemosa can balance hormonal imbalances and stimulate the reproductive system, leading to a return of menstrual cycles. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the reproductive organs, creating a conducive environment for regular menstruation.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: Isoferulic acid is believed to have estrogenic properties, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.
Actein: Actein has been shown to have a direct effect on the endocrine system, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to amenorrhea.
Cimicifugoside: Cimicifugoside is thought to have a regulatory effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis, which can help restore normal menstrual function and alleviate amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of bioactive compounds, including glycosides and phenolic acids, which are responsible for the medicinal properties.
Leaves: Although less commonly used, the leaves of Cimicifuga racemosa contain some bioactive compounds, although in smaller quantities compared to roots and rhizomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried cimicifuga racemosa roots and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Dry the cleaned cimicifuga racemosa roots in a dehydrator at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried cimicifuga racemosa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered cimicifuga racemosa with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain the extract of the chaste tree, which has been traditionally used to support menstrual health. The creams stimulate the pituitary gland to release hormones that regulate the menstrual cycle, leading to a more regular and balanced flow. By promoting hormonal balance, Vitex agnus-castus creams can help to induce menstrual periods in women with amenorrhea, a condition characterized by the absence of menstruation. This natural approach can be a helpful alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help regulate menstrual cycles by influencing the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, thus potentially stimulating menstrual flow in cases of amenorrhea.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These constituents may help regulate hormonal balance, particularly the luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) levels, which can aid in the return of menstrual cycles in individuals with amenorrhea.

Vitexin: This flavonoid glycoside has been found to have a positive effect on the menstrual cycle by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis and potentially influencing the levels of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH), thus aiding in the regulation of menstrual cycles in cases of amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for amenorrhea because they contain diterpenic acids, which have been shown to regulate hormonal balance and stimulate menstrual cycles.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for amenorrhea because they contain flavonoids, which have been found to have a stimulating effect on the menstrual cycle.
Roots: They are used to make creams for amenorrhea because they contain iridoids, which have been reported to have a regulating effect on the hormonal balance and menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried vitex agnus-castus berries from a reputable supplier or grow your own.
Combine the dried vitex agnus-castus berries with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 180-190°f for 30-40 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Stir in 10 milliliters of jojoba oil and 5 milliliters of sweet almond oil.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let it cool and harden for at least 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with amenorrhea because of its potential to regulate menstrual cycles and increase fertility. The herb's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, which is often a contributing factor to hormonal imbalances and amenorrhea. remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: They help with amenorrhea by regulating menstrual cycles and improving hormonal balance, particularly in cases of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
Saponins: They exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can contribute to amenorrhea, and also have a positive effect on the endocrine system.
Phenolic acids: They have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress, which may be a factor in amenorrhea, and also have a potential role in regulating hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are commonly used to make creams for amenorrhea due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to stimulate menstrual function.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Panax ginseng are used to make creams for amenorrhea due to their ability to promote blood circulation and stimulate hormone production.

Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng are used to make creams for amenorrhea due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried panax ginseng roots and rinse them under running water for 10 minutes.
Chop the roots into small pieces and soak them in 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 200 milliliters of distilled water.
Combine the liquid mixture with 100 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 30 minutes then remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rehmannia glutinosa creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain bioactive compounds that support hormone regulation and reproductive health. The cream's active ingredient, Rehmannia glutinosa, is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb known for its ability to tonify the kidney and liver, which are often implicated in menstrual irregularities. Iridoid Glycosides: Iridoid glycosides in Rehmannia glutinosa have been found to promote the growth and differentiation of endometrial cells, which can help to restore a normal menstrual cycle in individuals with amenorrhea.

Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins in Rehmannia glutinosa have been reported to have a regulatory effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, which can help to restore a normal menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rehmannia glutinosa are commonly used to make creams for amenorrhea because they contain various bioactive compounds that help regulate menstrual cycles.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Rehmannia glutinosa are used in creams to address amenorrhea due to their high content of compounds that stimulate uterine contractions and promote menstrual flow.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rehmannia glutinosa root and clean it thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Mix the dried root with 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 200g of distilled water and 10g of glycerin to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuscuta chinensis creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain the extract of Cuscuta chinensis, a traditional Chinese herb known for its uterine tonic properties. remedy_parts: Stems: Stems are used to make creams for amenorrhea due to their high concentration of saponins, which are believed to promote menstrual regularity.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried cuscuta chinensis root for its medicinal properties within 2 hours of sunrise.
Grind 20 grams of the harvested root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powder with 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 1 hour.
Add 5 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated within 20 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight to allow it to set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to stimulate the pituitary gland to release luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH). remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for amenorrhea due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and hormonal balancing properties.

Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for amenorrhea due to their ability to stimulate hormonal balance and menstrual cycle regulation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and mix it with 500g of distilled water to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 70-80°c to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 500g of distilled water to the liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter with 100g of the strained decoction in a double boiler.
Blend the mixture for 10 minutes at 45-50°c then pour it into jars and allow it to cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with amenorrhea because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that promote blood circulation and hormonal balance. The increased blood flow to the reproductive organs can stimulate ovulation and menstruation. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve endothelial function can enhance the body's ability to produce prostaglandins, which are essential for regulating menstrual cycles. This natural remedy can support women with amenorrhea, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and overall reproductive well-being. Regular use may also boost fertility.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help regulate menstrual cycles by improving blood flow to the reproductive organs and reducing oxidative stress.
Bilobalide: May help alleviate amenorrhea symptoms by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters involved in hormonal regulation, such as dopamine and serotonin.
Ginkgolides: May help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the reproductive system, which could contribute to the regulation of menstrual cycles and improvement of amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help with various health issues, including menstrual problems.
Seeds: Used in some herbal remedies, but their application in creams for amenorrhea is unclear; seeds might be used for their potential estrogenic effects.
Barks: Used to make creams due to their possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could aid in menstrual relief and overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall season.
Dry the harvested leaves in a cool dark place for 1-2 weeks to preserve their potency.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes to melt the wax and oil.
Strain the mixture and pour it into a container to cool and solidify into a rich cream.
